title: Control Theory Basics for Engineers
subtitle: An introduction to feedback loops and stability
body_html:
Introduction

Control theory is a branch of engineering that deals with the behavior of dynamical systems. A feedback loop is the fundamental building block. For more on stability analysis, see recent work by Smith 2023 and Lyapunov stability.

This connects to ideas in systems thinking and network science. We will explore how proportional-integral-derivative controllers can be tuned for robustness.

The mathematics behind Lyapunov functions provides a rigorous framework for analyzing stability in nonlinear systems. Engineers use these tools daily in robotics and aerospace applications.

title: Token Engineering and Mechanism Design
subtitle: Designing incentive structures for decentralized systems
body_html:
What is Token Engineering?

Token engineering applies systems engineering principles to the design of cryptoeconomic protocols. It draws on Control Theory Basics for Engineers to establish feedback mechanisms in token economies.

Key references include work by Vitalik Buterin on mechanism design and recent work by Smith 2023.

The field combines game theory, optimization, and distributed systems to create robust decentralized protocols.

title: Networks
subtitle: Understanding complex systems through graph theory

title: Systems Dynamics and Feedback
subtitle: Modeling complex adaptive systems
body_html:
Systems Dynamics

Building on Control Theory Basics for Engineers, we explore how feedback loops operate at the system level. Stock and flow diagrams help us model accumulation and depletion in complex systems.

See also Sterman's classic textbook for a comprehensive treatment. The field has applications in ecology, economics, and urban planning.

Agent-based models complement the differential equation approach by capturing heterogeneity and emergent behavior in sociotechnical systems.
